title: cytisus supinus f. calcareus
intro: Cytisus supinus f. calcareus is a rare and lesser-known subspecies of the Cytisus supinus plant, a type of shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to aid in the removal of mucus from the lungs. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some regions, it is also used to make a tincture or dried extract for medicinal purposes.
